title: TEA-Sim v2.1.0: Schema-first curation of personal health monitoring evidence
description: Reproducible reference implementation and evidence package for schema-first curation, payload minimisation, deterministic canonicalisation, signing and local receipt verification in synthetic personal health monitoring workflows. The package includes controlled mutation tests, property-based and finite bounded checks, an externally informed synthetic workload passage, figures and machine-readable results. It does not claim clinical validation, production deployment, legal compliance, FHIR/BALP conformance, global non-equivocation or complete formal verification.

creators.name: Clim, Antonio
creators.affiliation: Faculty of Cybernetics, Statistics and Economic Informatics, Bucharest University of Economic Studies

notes: Current version DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21318387. Previous version DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21226180. GitHub repository: https://github.com/antonioclim/TEA-Sim-TrustEvidence. GitHub release: https://github.com/antonioclim/TEA-Sim-TrustEvidence/releases/tag/v2.1.0. The workload is externally informed and synthetic; timing values are single-host descriptive observations.
